sourceMovie.title: A Taxi Driver
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto A Taxi Driver (2017) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Green Book
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If A Taxi Driver clicked for you, Green Book (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and history, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: October Sky
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If A Taxi Driver clicked for you, October Sky (1999) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and history, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after A Taxi Driver?
faq.answer: Try Green Book, October Sky, Antwone Fisher—each shares DNA with A Taxi Driver while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is A Taxi Driver?
faq.answer: A Taxi Driver (2017) is best described as Action, Drama, History. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is A Taxi Driver worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 8.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
